resources.string:
Knowmad-Specific Extended iCalendar Exchange Format, based on the iCalendar 2.0 standard defined in RFC 5545
## General Rules
- Each `VEVENT` does not require a `UID` - Must include the semester attribute `%1$s` - If the event belongs to a course, must include the course attribute `%5$s` - For recurrence rules: - Write an `RRULE` for the `VEVENT` to create recurring events - It is recommended to include an ending condition such as `COUNT` or `UNTIL`, and events occurring after the semester end time will not be added - Must set `DTSTART` and one ending indicator (`DURATION` or `DTEND`, choose one) - Only supports `VALARM` with `DISPLAY`
## Time Zone and Time
- Prefer using `TZID` to specify time zone - If no time zone is specified, use the `Z` suffix to indicate UTC time
## Required/Recommended VEVENT Properties
- Required: `DTSTART`, `%1$s` - At least one of: `DURATION`, `DTEND` - For regular events, required: `SUMMARY` (event name), `LOCATION`. In course events, they may be left empty to inherit the corresponding course\'s information unless special arrangements are made; manually specified values will override defaults - For course events, required: `%5$s` - Optional: `%9$s`, `RRULE`, `EXDATE`, `LOCATION`, `COLOR`, `VALARM`, `DESCRIPTION`, `PRIORITY`
## Custom Attributes
### %1$s
- Value: Semester ID, cannot be empty. If the user requests creating a semester, call the semester creation tool first to obtain the semester ID - Parameters — can be left empty if the semester ID points to an existing semester: - `LABEL`: Semester name - `%2$s`: Semester start date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) - `%3$s`: Semester end date (`YYYY-MM-DD`, exclusive; should be set to the day after the actual end) - `%4$s`: Semester time zone ID
Example (the sample UUID represents a default semester with no start/end dates):
``` %1$s;LABEL=My Semester;%2$s=2000-01-01;%3$s=2020-01-01;%4$s=Asia/Shanghai:019c0c33-1400-7225-a55f-906660045bdc ```
### %5$s
- Value: Course ID; leaving it empty indicates creating a new course - Parameters — can be left empty if the course ID points to an existing course: - `%6$s`: Semester ID; when scheduling a course, use the correct ID, or leave empty to use the default semester - `LABEL`: Course name - `%7$s`: Course instructor’s name - `%8$s`: Course location
Example (ID left empty):
``` %5$s;LABEL=My Course;%7$s=The Instructor;%8$s=The Room: ```
### %9$s
- Value: Text, instructor\'s name
